name: San Francisco
localTips: Get a Clipper card (or use it in your phone wallet) for Muni, BART and ferries — cable cars cost $8 a ride but are covered by a $13 day pass.
Dress in layers whatever the forecast says: the fog ('Karl' to locals) can drop temperatures 15 degrees between the Mission and Ocean Beach on the same afternoon.
Microclimates are real — when the coast is socked in, the Mission and Dolores Park are usually still sunny. Chase neighborhoods, not the forecast.
Never leave anything visible in a parked car, even for five minutes — break-ins are the city's signature petty crime.
Skip the cable car queue at Powell Street: walk a few stops up the line and board at any brown pole stop, hanging off the running board like locals do.

activities.title: Walk the Golden Gate Bridge
activities.description: 1.7 miles of International Orange from the Welcome Center to Vista Point — walk out at least to the first tower and feel the deck hum under the traffic.

activities.title: Lands End Coastal Trail & Sutro Baths
activities.description: Cypress-lined cliffs, shipwreck views, the ruins of the 1896 Sutro Baths and a hidden labyrinth at Eagle Point — the wildest corner of any American city.

activities.title: Ferry Building Marketplace
activities.description: Oysters at Hog Island, Acme bread and Cowgirl Creamery under the 1898 clock tower — plus the famous farmers market spilling onto the bayfront on Saturdays.

activities.title: Mission murals: Balmy & Clarion alleys
activities.description: Two alleys of wall-to-wall political and psychedelic murals a few blocks apart, the open-air galleries of the Mission's Chicano art tradition since the 1970s.

activities.title: Golden Gate Park ramble
activities.description: Bigger than Central Park: the Conservatory of Flowers' white Victorian glasshouse, Stow Lake's pedal boats, bison paddock and the roar of Ocean Beach at the far end.

activities.title: Chinatown: Grant Avenue & Stockton Street
activities.description: The oldest Chinatown in North America — dragon lampposts on Grant for the postcards, then Stockton Street's produce markets and dim sum counters for the real thing.

activities.title: City Lights Books & North Beach
activities.description: Ferlinghetti's 1953 bookstore where the Beats were published, then espresso at Caffe Trieste and a wander through Little Italy's neon-lit blocks.

activities.title: Lombard Street & Russian Hill stairways
activities.description: The eight hairpin turns of the 'crookedest street', then the hidden Macondray Lane stairs — the leafy pedestrian lane that inspired Tales of the City.

activities.title: Twin Peaks lookout after dark
activities.description: The 922-foot summit with the full grid of city lights, the Bay Bridge's LED sculpture and Sutro Tower looming behind — the definitive night panorama.

activities.title: Musée Mécanique penny arcade
activities.description: Three hundred working antique arcade machines at Fisherman's Wharf — feed quarters to Laffing Sal, hand-cranked mutoscopes and a steam-powered motorcycle.

activities.title: Crissy Field shoreline walk
activities.description: A flat bayside path from the Marina to Fort Point, the Golden Gate Bridge growing bigger with every step — windsurfers, dogs and picnic tables on the old airfield.

activities.title: Dolores Park people-watching
activities.description: The Mission's sunny living room — skyline views from the upper corner, picnics, and a weekend scene that's half festival, half neighborhood reunion.

activities.title: Palace of Fine Arts rotunda
activities.description: The last survivor of the 1915 world's fair — a Roman-scale rotunda and colonnade reflected in a swan lagoon, absurdly romantic at dusk.

activities.title: The Mission burrito pilgrimage
activities.description: The foil-wrapped, rice-and-beans behemoth was invented here — settle the La Taqueria vs El Farolito argument yourself, a few blocks apart on Mission Street.
activities.insiderNote: At La Taqueria order it 'dorado style' (griddled crispy, off-menu); El Farolito's counter runs until well after midnight for the post-bar crowd.

activities.title: Counter seats at Swan Oyster Depot
activities.description: Eighteen marble counter stools, fourth-generation shuckers, and the freshest crab Louie in California — unchanged on Polk Street since 1912.
activities.insiderNote: It's cash only and the line forms before the 10:30am open — go solo or as a pair mid-morning on a weekday and you'll be seated in minutes. Closes at 5:30pm.

activities.title: Tonga Room's indoor rainstorms
activities.description: A tiki palace in the Fairmont's basement built around the hotel's 1929 swimming pool — the band plays on a floating barge while thunderstorms erupt on schedule.
activities.insiderNote: Happy hour (Wed–Fri from 5pm) gets you the same rainstorms with cheaper mai tais and an all-you-can-eat bar bites deal — book a table, walk-ins queue.

activities.title: 16th Avenue Tiled Steps & Grandview Park
activities.description: A 163-step mosaic river of sea-to-stars tilework climbing through the Inner Sunset, with a second hidden staircase continuing to a 360° summit dune park.
activities.insiderNote: Keep climbing past the famous steps up Moraga to Grandview Park — most visitors stop at the mosaic and miss the far better view. Clearest before the afternoon fog rolls in.

activities.title: Golden Gate Fortune Cookie Factory
activities.description: In Chinatown's Ross Alley, two women fold cookies by hand over iron griddles the way it's been done since 1962 — warm, flat 'fortune-less' rejects are the treat.
activities.insiderNote: Buy the bag of flat unfolded cookies — better than the folded ones — and bring a dollar if you want to photograph the folding station.

activities.title: The Wave Organ at high tide
activities.description: An acoustic sculpture of pipes and salvaged cemetery marble on a Marina jetty — the bay literally gurgles and sighs through it, with bridge and Alcatraz views thrown in.
activities.insiderNote: It only really 'plays' within an hour or two of high tide — check a tide table before walking out past the yacht club. Sunset high tides are the jackpot.

activities.title: Blues at The Saloon, SF's oldest bar
activities.description: Pouring since 1861 and a survivor of the 1906 fire, this gloriously scuffed North Beach room has live blues every single night in a space the size of a rail car.
activities.insiderNote: Legend says firefighters saved it in 1906 to protect the whiskey. Small cover on weekends, cash for the band's tip jar — afternoon sets on weekends are free.

activities.title: Morning croissant at Arsicault Bakery
activities.description: A tiny Richmond District bakery whose croissants made Bon Appétit's 'best in America' — shatteringly laminated, best eaten warm on the walk to Golden Gate Park.
activities.insiderNote: The original on Arguello often sells out of almond croissants by 10am on weekends — the newer Civic Center location has the same pastry and half the line.

activities.title: Hawk Hill above the fog line
activities.description: A Marin Headlands summit staring straight down the Golden Gate's towers — when Karl the Fog swallows the city, you're often standing above it in sunshine.
activities.insiderNote: Drive Conzelman Road until it turns one-way; foggy summer evenings are exactly when to go — the bridge towers poke through the white river below. Bring a real jacket.

activities.title: Alcatraz Island & cellhouse audio tour
activities.description: The ferry across, then the superb cell-by-cell audio tour narrated by former guards and inmates — book weeks ahead; the night tour is even eerier.
activities.bookingQuery: Alcatraz Island tour ticket San Francisco

activities.title: SFMOMA
activities.description: Seven floors from Warhol to the Fisher Collection's Calders, a living wall, and Richard Serra's walk-through steel — America's biggest modern art museum.

activities.title: Exploratorium on Pier 15
activities.description: Six hundred hands-on exhibits on a bayfront pier — the museum that invented the science museum, capped by the pitch-black Tactile Dome crawl.

activities.title: California Academy of Sciences
activities.description: A rainforest dome, an albino alligator, a planetarium and an aquarium under one living roof in Golden Gate Park — the Thursday NightLife edition adds cocktails.
activities.bookingQuery: California Academy of Sciences ticket San Francisco

activities.title: Golden Gate bay cruise
activities.description: An hour on the water: under the Golden Gate Bridge, around Alcatraz and back along the city front — the skyline finally makes sense from the bay.
activities.bookingQuery: San Francisco bay cruise Golden Gate Bridge Alcatraz

activities.title: Muir Woods & Sausalito half-day tour
activities.description: Cathedral-quiet old-growth redwoods 12 miles from downtown, then Mediterranean-style Sausalito for lunch — ferry back across the bay if you time it right.
activities.bookingQuery: Muir Woods and Sausalito tour from San Francisco

activities.title: Napa & Sonoma wine country day trip
activities.description: Three tastings across Napa and Sonoma with a designated driver handling Highway 29 — cellars, vineyard picnics and golden valley light an hour north.
activities.bookingQuery: Napa Sonoma wine country day tour from San Francisco

activities.title: Bike the bridge to Sausalito
activities.description: Rent a bike at Fisherman's Wharf, roll through Crissy Field, over the Golden Gate Bridge and coast downhill into Sausalito — return by ferry with the skyline ahead.
activities.bookingQuery: bike rental Golden Gate Bridge to Sausalito San Francisco

activities.title: A night at SFJAZZ Center
activities.description: The first purpose-built jazz hall in America — steep, intimate seating in Hayes Valley where legends and rising stars play 300 nights a year.
activities.bookingQuery: SFJAZZ Center concert tickets San Francisco

activities.title: Cobb's Comedy Club or Punch Line
activities.description: San Francisco's two storied comedy rooms — Cobb's in North Beach and the Punch Line downtown, where Robin Williams and Dave Chappelle worked out material.
activities.bookingQuery: Cobb's Comedy Club tickets San Francisco

activities.title: Giants game at Oracle Park
activities.description: Baseball on the bay — kayakers waiting for splash hits in McCovey Cove, garlic fries, and the best ballpark view in the majors.
activities.bookingQuery: San Francisco Giants tickets Oracle Park
